task_completion.description: Create a multi-sheet inventory optimization workbook.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an open spreadsheet application (like LibreOffice Calc) showing a file named 'inventory_optimization.xlsx' with at least 3 distinct sheet tabs at the bottom (e.g., Data, Analysis, Dashboard).

task_completion.description: Perform ABC revenue classification for the 150 SKUs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a column in the spreadsheet explicitly assigning 'A', 'B', or 'C' to SKUs based on revenue. There should be exactly 29 'A' class items if a summary count is visible.

task_completion.description: Calculate inventory turnover ratios and identify dead stock.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for columns or data fields labeled 'Turnover Ratio' (or similar) and 'Dead Stock' (or a flag for 0 shipments in the last 90 days). A count of 72 dead stock items might be visible.

task_completion.description: Compute reorder points and safety stock levels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for columns in the spreadsheet analysis containing calculated values for 'Reorder Point' (ROP) and 'Safety Stock'.

task_completion.description: Create a summary dashboard showing total revenue and class distribution.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a dedicated 'Dashboard' or 'Summary' sheet displaying aggregated metrics, specifically a 'Total Revenue' figure and a breakdown/distribution of the A, B, and C classes (e.g., a table or chart showing the count of SKUs in each class).

task_completion.description: Include dead stock value on the summary dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a specific metric or cell on the summary dashboard explicitly labeled 'Dead Stock Value' (or similar) showing the total financial value of the identified dead stock.

integrity.description: The agent used appropriate tools (spreadsheet GUI or data analysis scripts) to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the use of a spreadsheet application (e.g., LibreOffice Calc) or a code editor/terminal running a Python script (e.g., pandas) to process the CSVs and generate the Excel file. It should not be a plain text file manually renamed to .xlsx.

integrity.description: The agent calculated the metrics rather than hardcoding fake data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the dashboard metrics and classifications appear to be derived from the 150 SKUs and ~2500 transactions (e.g., formulas in the formula bar, or a script that performs actual math) rather than manually typed placeholder text.

integrity.description: The output file is saved in the correct directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the file manager, terminal save commands, or spreadsheet 'Save As' dialog to confirm the file is saved exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/Spreadsheets/inventory_optimization.xlsx'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/Spreadsheets/inventory_optimization.xlsx' and contain a minimum of 3 sheets. The dataset contains exactly 150 SKUs and 2493 transactions. If the agent calculated correctly, there will be exactly 29 'A' class SKUs (top 80% revenue) and exactly 72 dead stock SKUs (zero shipments in the last 90 days).
